original_description: Force a structured thinking & validation pass before writing ANY production code, scaffolding, or pseudo-code for a new feature, subsystem, architecture decision, library choice, refactor plan, or data-model change. Use this skill aggressively — whenever the user says things like "I want to build / add / implement / design / refactor / migrate / introduce / support X", "how should we structure Y", "which approach for Z", "add a new endpoint/service/module/skill/agent", "set up …", or any request that contains a design component — even when the user does NOT explicitly say "plan first". Also use when a bug fix turns out to hide a design decision (3+ substantively different fixes). The skill turns rough intent into a validated, approved plan in a fixed five-section schema; it never produces code itself. User-facing output is in Traditional Chinese (繁體中文).
best_description: Force a structured thinking & validation pass before writing ANY production code, scaffolding, or pseudo-code for a new feature, subsystem, architecture decision, library choice, refactor plan, or data-model change. Use this skill aggressively — whenever the user says things like "I want to build / add / implement / design / refactor / migrate / introduce / support X", "how should we structure Y", "which approach for Z", "add a new endpoint/service/module/skill/agent", "set up …", or any request that contains a design component — even when the user does NOT explicitly say "plan first". Also use when a bug fix turns out to hide a design decision (3+ substantively different fixes). The skill turns rough intent into a validated, approved plan in a fixed five-section schema; it never produces code itself. User-facing output is in Traditional Chinese (繁體中文).
final_description: Use this skill when the user wants a written plan, design doc, proposal, or recommendation BEFORE any code — scope, non-goals, risks, trade-offs, or key decisions captured as an artifact to review, approve, or share with a team. Trigger on intents like: "plan for X", "design doc", "walk me through how you'd approach", "help me think through", "before we start building", "don't write code yet", "align the team", "green-light", "approve first", build-vs-buy or library choices, monolith-to-microservice splits, auth/database/framework migrations, new-service designs, introducing patterns (event sourcing, CQRS, sagas), multi-file refactors, data-model changes. Also 繁體中文 (設計、規劃、範圍、風險、關鍵決策、批准、不要直接寫). The deliverable is deliberation — thinking, judgment, a document — never code. Do NOT trigger when what-to-do is decided and only execution remains: bug fixes, typos, renames, adding a specific test, running commands, env repair.

history.description: Force a structured thinking & validation pass before writing ANY production code, scaffolding, or pseudo-code for a new feature, subsystem, architecture decision, library choice, refactor plan, or data-model change. Use this skill aggressively — whenever the user says things like "I want to build / add / implement / design / refactor / migrate / introduce / support X", "how should we structure Y", "which approach for Z", "add a new endpoint/service/module/skill/agent", "set up …", or any request that contains a design component — even when the user does NOT explicitly say "plan first". Also use when a bug fix turns out to hide a design decision (3+ substantively different fixes). The skill turns rough intent into a validated, approved plan in a fixed five-section schema; it never produces code itself. User-facing output is in Traditional Chinese (繁體中文).

train_results.query: 想把 billing module 從 monolith 拆出來做 microservice, 不要直接開始寫. 先給我完整的設計思路, 範圍, 風險, 跟關鍵決策. 我要批准後才開始實作

plan for introducing event sourcing to our order management module. i need scope, non-goals, risks, and the key architectural decisions written down before any coding. im not ready to implement, just to decide

help me think through whether to adopt temporal.io or build our own saga pattern on top of postgres. give me a concrete recommendation with reasoning and what evidence would change your mind — not a both-sides writeup

before i green-light the refactor of our permissions system (currently spread across 6 django apps), i need a written plan with what's in scope, what's explicitly out, and the 3-5 key decisions i'm signing off on
this test in tests/test_auth.py line 127 is failing because the mock returns None but the assertion expects {}, just add a null check and move on
add a unit test for the parseDate function in utils/date.ts, should cover the ISO string case on line 23 and the empty string case. the function itself is fine, just needs coverage

I need a proper design doc before we start building the new payments service. rails 7, stripe connect, b2b marketplace model. draft the plan — scope, out-of-scope, key decisions, unknowns. don't write code yet, i need to align the team first
set up my local dev env per the README, something broke when i pulled latest main and now pytest wont even collect. just need it working again
walk me through how you'd approach migrating our user auth from devise sessions to stateless jwt. 800k active users, live app. I want a written plan I can show my team before any code gets touched
theres a typo in README.md line 34, says 'baranus' instead of 'baransu'. fix it
run the test suite and tell me whats red, then fix any obvious assertion-mismatch bugs. no big changes
refactor user_service.py variable naming — all the names are camelCase but the rest of the repo is snake_case. purely cosmetic, no logic changes

test_results.query: our oncall has been paged 3x/week for the same stripe webhook double-processing issue. im done patching. lets step back and redesign this properly — i want a written proposal with scope, key decisions, and risks

we're evaluating snowflake vs bigquery vs databricks for our new analytics warehouse. 5TB/month ingest growing 2x/year, need a clear recommendation with falsifiable criteria. no both-sides waffle, pick one and tell me why
thinking about adding graphql alongside our existing rest api for the mobile clients. before I commit I need the trade-offs and a recommended approach laid out as a plan — no code, just the thinking
migrate utils/date_helpers.js to typescript. just converting, no behavior change, just adding type annotations. no design decisions needed

i want to add multi-tenancy to our b2b saas. currently single-tenant postgres, 5 engineers, 3-month budget. give me the approach, scope, key decisions — then i'll decide whether to greenlight. no code yet
can you explain how our auth middleware was designed? i inherited this codebase from the previous team and want to understand why they chose jwt over sessions. not planning any changes, just learning
can you review the PR i just opened, #482, its 3 files around the retry logic. just checking for bugs, dont rewrite it
rename the variable userEmail to userEmailAddress across the whole codebase, its inconsistent between files right now. mechanical change, no logic shifts

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants a written plan or design doc BEFORE any code — especially when they say "don't write code yet", "I need to align the team", "give me a plan to approve", "walk me through your approach", "help me think through X", or want "scope, risks, and key decisions" written down. Trigger for: new features or services; architecture and library/vendor choices (build vs. buy); refactors spanning multiple files; migrations (auth, database, monolith-to-microservice, framework upgrades); data-model changes; any request signalling deliberation and approval, not implementation. Also trigger when a bug fix reveals 3+ fundamentally different fixes with real trade-offs. Produces an approved plan; never writes code. Do NOT trigger for straightforward bug fixes, typos, test additions, running commands, or renames where the "what" is decided and only execution remains.

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants to think, plan, design, or decide BEFORE any code is written. Trigger on requests for: a written plan, design doc, scope/non-goals/risks writeup, architectural recommendation, build-vs-buy decision, migration strategy, refactor plan, or approach walkthrough. Trigger on deliberation signals: "don't write code yet", "before we start building", "align the team", "approve first", "green-light", "written plan", "design doc", "scope and risks", "key decisions", "walk me through how you'd approach", "help me think through", "plan for introducing X", "adopt X or build our own". Trigger for new features/services, architecture or library choices, multi-file refactors, auth/database/framework migrations, monolith-to-microservice splits, data-model changes, and event-sourcing introductions. Also handles 繁體中文 equivalents (設計、規劃、範圍、風險、關鍵決策、不要直接寫). Do NOT trigger for bug fixes, typos, renames, test additions, or commands where only execution remains.

history.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants a plan, design doc, or written proposal BEFORE implementation — not the code itself. This is the go-to skill for any request where the deliverable is thinking, not typing: architecture proposals, migration strategies, refactor plans, build-vs-buy decisions, new-service designs, data-model changes, introducing patterns like event sourcing or CQRS, or "walk me through how you'd approach X". Strong signals include: "plan", "design doc", "scope", "non-goals", "risks", "key decisions", "approve", "green-light", "align the team", "before we start", "don't write code yet", "think through", "recommendation with reasoning", plus 繁體中文 equivalents (設計、規劃、範圍、風險、關鍵決策、批准、不要直接寫). If the user is asking for judgment, trade-offs, or a written artifact they can show teammates — trigger. Do NOT trigger when the what-to-do is already decided and only execution remains: bug fixes, typos, renames, adding a specific test, running commands, or local env repair.
